content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://aier.org/article/the-economics-of-the-minimum-wage-myths-facts-and-consequences/", "source_title": "The Economics of the Minimum Wage: Myths, Facts, and ... - AIER", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "New Zealand was the first country to implement a national minimum wage (1894), followed by Australia (1907).", "direct_quote": "New Zealand was the first country to implement a national minimum wage (1894), followed by Australia (1907).", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "In the United States, the minimum wage became law with the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) of 1938, which set a national wage floor of $0.25...", "direct_quote": "In the United States, the minimum wage became law with the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) of 1938, which set a national wage floor of $0.25 per hour (about $5.50 in 2024 dollars).", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Certain employers may be exempt from minimum wage laws, such as small businesses with fewer than a specified number of employees, those hiri...", "direct_quote": "Certain employers may be exempt from minimum wage laws, such as small businesses with fewer than a specified number of employees, those hiring seasonal or agricultural workers, and family-owned businesses where only immediate relatives are employed.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
